identitySentence: Lewis, Passons & Darnell, PC is a Denton County family law firm where both named partners are Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization.

concept.headline: Board-Certified Family Law Counsel, Right Here in Denton
concept.subheadline: Divorce, custody, and adoption decisions are hard enough without wondering whether your attorney has done this before. At Lewis, Passons & Darnell, P.C., both named partners are Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization, and your first consultation starts with straight answers about your options.
credibilityPoints: Both named partners are Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization
Managing Partner Andrew Passons has practiced law in Texas since 2001 and has held board certification in family law since 2010
Partner Sarah Darnell has practiced law in Texas since 2006 and has held board certification in family law since 2012
Denton office on South Carroll Boulevard, with a Celina satellite office available by appointment

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: Lewis, Passons & Darnell, P.C. handles the full range of Texas family law, including divorce, child custody and visitation, complex property division, child support enforcement, post-divorce modification, adoption, and paternity. For families who want to resolve disputes outside the courtroom, the firm also offers mediation and collaborative law.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The firm practices from its office at 509 S. Carroll Blvd. in Denton and serves families across Denton County and North Texas, with a Celina satellite office available by appointment. Its lineage runs through the Law Office of Mark Lewis, whose roughly four decades of Denton practice preceded the firm's current name.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your consultation is a conversation, not a commitment. The firm will ask about your family situation, your goals, and any immediate concerns, then give you an honest assessment of your options and what moving forward would involve.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Dear Lewis, Passons & Darnell, P.C.,

Your firm holds a credential most family law practices in Denton County cannot claim: both named partners are Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization, Andrew Passons since 2010 and Sarah Darnell since 2012.

People facing divorce, custody disputes, or adoption decisions often reach out in the evening and from a phone. Your website already gives those visitors strong reasons to trust you, from your attorney bios to your testimonials. One thing it cannot do is let a motivated visitor reserve a consultation time and commit to keeping it; today the paths in are a phone call during office hours or a general contact form. That is the gap a Lead Capturer closes.

We built one specifically for your firm. It opens with a first impression written for Lewis, Passons & Darnell, P.C.: 'Lewis, Passons & Darnell, PC is a Denton County family law firm where both named partners are Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization.' Behind it sits a custom contact form shaped around your practice areas, from divorce and child custody to adoption, mediation, and collaborative law.

Here is what happens when someone reaches out. The inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox, with the sender's address in Reply-To. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to the topic of their message, whether that is custody, property division, or child support. That response presents your available consultation times, and a $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so your calendar reflects committed consultations rather than maybes.

We would like you to judge this on evidence, not promises. We are offering a 15-day proof period so you can test the complete experience end to end, from first inquiry to confirmed appointment, before deciding anything.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information anywhere in the form.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to your TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm that response is sent to the customer email with no action from your staff.
9. Confirm the customer inbox actually receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual topic of the inquiry.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select one of the offered times.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter type.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In the payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us.

We would welcome the chance to set this up for your review. Fifteen days, one demo, and you will know exactly what it does for your intake.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
